name: Order Log
creator: JD
description: Logs into ownerville, downloads the ORDER LOG tab from the ATT Tracker 2.1 - D2D Tableau workbook (filtered to Rafael Hidalgo, last month → today), and drops a polished color-coded .xlsx in your Downloads folder ready to paste into Slack.

checklist.text: Make sure your internet connection is stable — the script logs into Cloudflare-protected ownerville so a flaky connection breaks the run

The script opens its OWN Chrome window automatically and signs into ownerville + Tableau on its own. Leave that window alone while it runs (about 2 min). Do NOT open Reporting Chrome for this one — it uses a separate stealth Chrome (patchright).

breakdown:
WHAT IT DOES
Logs into ownerville → opens ATT Tracker 2.1 - D2D in Tableau → ORDER LOG tab → filters to **Rafael Hidalgo** + last month-to-today → downloads the Crosstab CSV → color-codes + sorts → saves a polished `.xlsx` to your Downloads folder.

WHEN IT RUNS
Daily at 7:30 AM (per JD's wire-up). Assigned to JD.

WHERE THE OUTPUT GOES
`~/Downloads/` — file name starts with `Order Log`. Open Downloads in Finder/Explorer + drag into Slack when done.

FIRST-TIME SETUP
The deps (`patchright`, `pandas`, `openpyxl`, `python-dateutil`) are in the project's `requirements.txt` and auto-install on launcher restart. Patchright also needs Chromium installed once per machine — the launcher doesn't do this yet, so the first run on a new machine may fail until you run:
`./.venv/bin/patchright install chromium` (macOS / Linux)
`.venv\Scripts\patchright install chromium` (Windows)
followup_notes: Hardcoded credentials in the script — flagged to Megan for migration to env-var-based secrets in a follow-up commit.
